title: Jock itch
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Jock itch is a fungal infection of the groin, also called tinea cruris.
It can cause an itchy or burning rash in the groin or upper inner thigh.
Keeping the area clean and dry and using clinician-recommended antifungal treatment can help uncomplicated cases.
Secondary infection, genital or mucosal diagnostic uncertainty, immunocompromise, and recurrent or extensive disease need separate handling.
